title: 10 best herbal capsules for gastritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with gastritis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and heal the stomach lining. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, licorice capsules can help to protect the stomach mucosa from damage caused by stomach acid, promoting a healthy digestive environment and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the stomach lining associated with gastritis.
Licoric Acid: As a natural antacid, licoric acid can help neutralize stomach acid and relieve symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and indigestion caused by gastritis.
Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to have protective effects on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and pain in the stomach lining, alleviating gastritis symptoms.

Flavonoids (particularly quercetin): As antioxidants, flavonoids help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing gastritis inflammation.
Licoricidin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, alleviating gastritis symptoms and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining, making them effective in treating gastritis.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate gastritis symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the plant contains glycosides and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the stomach lining and reduce gastritis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier and sift out impurities.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500g of gelatin and 50g of glycerin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of 2g of the powder and 1g of the gelatin mixture for 30 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with gastritis because they contain soothing compounds that calm inflammation and reduce irritation in the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile work to ease symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and cramps associated with gastritis. Additionally, chamomile's natural antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and discomfort. As a result, chamomile capsules provide effective relief from gastritis-related discomfort, promoting a sense of calm and digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation and swelling in the stomach lining, which helps to alleviate symptoms of gastritis such as nausea and abdominal pain.
Antacid properties: The soothing and calming effects of chamomile help to neutralize stomach acid and relieve heartburn and indigestion associated with gastritis.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with gastritis by reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate gastritis symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.

Matricaria chamomilla essential oil (containing bisabolol): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm the stomach and reduce inflammation associated with gastritis, providing relief from symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part for treating gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for gastritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Seeds: They are sometimes used to make capsules for gastritis due to their potential to help soothe and calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a clean and dry environment.
Dry the harvested matricaria chamomilla flowers in a low temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 2 grams of cellulose and 1 gram of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a powder funnel and capsule filler for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with gastritis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the stomach. The active compound gingerol in ginger capsules has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause pain and swelling in the stomach lining. Additionally, ginger's natural carminative properties help to expel gas and ease bloating, providing relief from gastritis symptoms such as abdominal discomfort and nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramps, and abdominal pain.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: Herbal ginger capsules contain enzymes that stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, helping to break down food more efficiently and reducing discomfort associated with gastritis.

Antioxidant properties: Ginger's high antioxidant content helps protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate gastritis symptoms like heartburn, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with gastritis, such as pain and swelling in the stomach lining.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also sesquiterpene compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in gastritis patients.

Zingibain: This proteolytic enzyme has anti-inflammatory and anti-ulcer properties, which help break down excess mucus and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, thus alleviating symptoms of gastritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Zingiber officinale Roots are also widely used, as they contain essential oils and other bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining in gastritis patients.

Stems: Although less commonly used than Rhizomes and Roots, Zingiber officinale Stems are sometimes utilized due to their ability to absorb and store nutrients, which can contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a sharp knife or garden tool in late summer.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently brushing off dirt with a soft brush for 10 minutes.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10-20 grams of cellulose or vegetable cellulose in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 0-00 capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves by cutting them at the base of the plant with scissors or a knife.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare material in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 100 milligrams of the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 50 milligrams of filler material like starch or cellulose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with gastritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, which effectively soothes and calms the stomach lining. This natural remedy reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, alleviating symptoms such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain commonly associated with gastritis. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage, promoting a healthy gut environment that facilitates proper digestion and absorption of nutrients.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric capsules' curcumin content helps reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which is a primary cause of gastritis symptoms such as pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant Activity: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage stomach tissue and contribute to gastritis, helping to promote healing and protect against further irritation.

Gastroprotective Compounds: Certain compounds in turmeric, such as curcuminoids, have been shown to directly protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, ulcers, and other irritants, which can exacerbate gastritis.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach lining, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.

Tumerone: Tumerone, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from gastritis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another curcuminoid, has shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, thereby alleviating gastritis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also used for their curcumin content, which is believed to aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.
Buds: Buds of the Curcuma longa plant are sometimes used due to their high antioxidant content, which may help protect the stomach from damage and promote recovery from gastritis.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 250ml of water and 10g of gelatin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly for 10 minutes or until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine and 500mg per capsule.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, aloe leaf extracts are often used to soothe and calm gastrointestinal issues associated with gastritis.

Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of bioactive compounds, rhyzome extracts have been traditionally used to treat digestive disorders such as gastritis, promoting gut health and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera gel from fresh leaves and filter it through cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Mix the filtered aloe vera gel with 200mg of dried ginger powder and 100mg of peppermint leaf extract.
Add 500ml of distilled water to the mixture and heat it gently at 60°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Allow the mixture to cool and strain it through a coffee filter to remove any sediment or impurities.
Fill empty capsules with the cooled mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram capsules helps with gastritis because they contain the herb Origanum majorana, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, bloating, and nausea. Additionally, wild marjoram's antimicrobial properties may help to eliminate Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. By reducing inflammation and eliminating bacterial infections, herbal wild marjoram capsules can provide relief from gastritis symptoms and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wild marjoram contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, reducing pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with gastritis.

Gastroprotective properties: Wild marjoram's bioactive compounds have been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid, bile, and other irritants, thereby preventing further inflammation and healing existing lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild marjoram plant are used to make medicinal capsules for gastritis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues.

Flowers: Wild marjoram flowers are used in medicinal capsules for gastritis due to their ability to reduce inflammation, relax spasms, and provide relief from symptoms such as heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh oregano leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried origanum vulgare flowers.
Clean and dry the harvested oregano to remove any dirt or moisture using a clean cloth.
Grind 10-20 grams of dried origanum vulgare flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400-500 mg of the ground oregano powder with 200-300 mg of vegetable cellulose capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the oregano-capsule mixture, yielding approximately 50-60 capsules per batch.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen capsules helps with gastritis because they contain methyl salicylate, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain associated with gastritis. Additionally, wintergreen's antimicrobial properties may help to eliminate Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis, further contributing to its beneficial effects on digestive health.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in wintergreen capsules help neutralize free radicals and protect the stomach lining from damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate gastritis and other digestive issues.

Pain-relieving properties: Wintergreen's salicylic acid content provides natural pain relief for individuals suffering from gastritis-related discomfort, such as heartburn, bloating, and abdominal pain, allowing for improved quality of life.

Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues like gastritis due to their ability to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems.
Crush the collected plant material into small pieces weighing 200g.
Mix the crushed material with 400ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the essence.
Fill gelatin capsules with the extracted essence and seal immediately.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and luteolin, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms of gastritis such as pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of purple coneflower may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gastritis and its associated symptoms.
Gastric mucosa protection: Purple coneflower's saponin content has been shown to strengthen the gastric mucosa, reducing the risk of ulcers and other damage that can exacerbate gastritis.

Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastritis, due to their ability to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and chop 20-30 grams of the harvested flowers into small pieces.
Combine chopped flowers with 500ml of boiling water and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl and discard solids.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with 250mg of the cooled liquid extract and seal immediately.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with gastritis because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds called polyphenols, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The capsules also possess antimicrobial properties that combat Helicobacter pylori bacteria, a common cause of gastritis. Additionally, purple coneflower contains antioxidants that soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with gastritis. As a result, herbal purple coneflower capsules can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and abdominal pain.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for gastritis are a natural alternative to conventional treatments, providing relief from digestive discomforts caused by inflammation in the stomach lining. These capsules contain a blend of herbs such as slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root that soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing acid reflux and abdominal pain. For instance, products like Gastrilax and Gastro-Plus have helped many individuals alleviate symptoms, allowing them to enjoy their favorite foods without discomfort and improving overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastritis that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive issues such as ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal capsules containing extracts from plants like turmeric, ginger, and slippery elm may help alleviate symptoms like abdominal pain, bloating, and inflammation. Other conditions, including acid reflux, stomach cramps, and diarrhea, can also be managed with herbal supplements, providing a natural alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with gastritis is a blend of slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root. Slippery elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes, while licorice root reduces inflammation and calms digestive issues. Marshmallow root adds its anti-inflammatory properties and helps to heal the stomach lining. A balanced ratio of these herbs can effectively alleviate gastritis symptoms, promoting a healthy digestive system and reducing discomfort. This combination is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat gastritis and related digestive issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, herbal capsules may be beneficial for gastritis due to their efficacy in treating gastrointestinal disorders. The study found that among 1137 patients, 10.4% used phytopreparations for GI disorders, which were well tolerated with no major side effects and were effective in treating mild and moderate GI disorders. The most frequently used preparations, such as Iberogast, contained herbal ingredients like artichoke and silymarin, which may help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
